article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
obligation_text: Deployers of high-risk AI systems shall keep the logs automatically generated by that high-risk AI system to the extent such logs are under their control, for a period appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, of at least six months, unless provided otherwise in applicable Union or national law.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system log management requirements, including log retention policies, control assessment, retention period determination based on intended purpose, and compliance with applicable Union and national law retention requirements.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system log management requirements, including log retention policies, control assessment, retention period determination based on intended purpose, and compliance with applicable Union and national law retention requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system documentation standards, including model cards, system cards, datasheets for datasets, and the evaluation evidence standards required for regulatory conformity assessment.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 26(6) (competency) superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.511, cosine: 0.603). Secondary: AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.595), SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.590).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.1/10 (raw 0.511). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessment, knowledge, system]. EU AI Act competency requirement terms [applicable, based, compliance, control, determination, intended] vs WIDAI terms [cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.876, R=0.878). Balanced P/R — the 30-word EU AI Act competency and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Semantic tension between "Knowledge of AI system log management requirements, including log retention poli" and "Knowledge of AI system documentation standards, including model cards, system ca". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.603 > STS 0.511 (gap +0.092). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, assessment, knowledge, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 18.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessment, knowledge, system]. EU AI Act-only: [applicable, based, compliance, control, determination, intended]. WIDAI-only: [cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, documentation, evaluation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 100.0% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.511 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
STS 0.511 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
